turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Participating in Group Decisions and Negotiating Responsibilities

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay… let’s do role play. You are my teammate. I want to talk about group project.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, sure. I’m your teammate. Let’s talk about the group project. What’s on your mind?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Um… I feel our project is not moving. We didn’t decide roles yet.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yeah, I feel that too. We’ve talked a bit, but nothing is really decided. What do you think is the main problem right now?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Everyone waits. No one wants to start. So we are just stuck.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I see what you mean. If no one starts, it’s hard to move forward. Do you feel uncomfortable bringing this up in the group chat, or in person?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Both… I don’t want sound bossy. I’m worried people feel bad.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That makes sense. I don’t think you’d sound bossy if you explain it carefully. What were you thinking of saying?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I want say… Maybe we can decide roles first. But I don’t know how to say nicely.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, how about something like: “Maybe we can decide roles first so we know what to work on”? That sounds more like a suggestion than an order. How does that feel?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: That sounds better. Less strong.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good. And do you already know which part you’d like to do, or are you still deciding?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think I prefer research part. Writing is okay. But presentation… I’m not confident.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s fair. You could say that you’re happy to focus on research and writing, and ask if someone else feels comfortable presenting. If no one volunteers, we can think about sharing it. Would that be okay?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes… If we share, I feel less scared.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That makes sense. Also, maybe we can set a small deadline, like finishing research by next week, so we don’t leave everything until the end.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Next week… You mean end of next week?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, by the end of next week. We can check in again after that and see how everyone is going.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. That sounds reasonable.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So maybe you can send a message suggesting roles and a small deadline, and we’ll see how the others respond.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay… I feel more confident now. I think I can send message.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good. If you want, I can also help you think about the exact message later.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Thank you. This role play helped me.
